# CGColorRenderingIntent.saturation | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ Core Graphics ](/documentation/coregraphics)

-  CGColorRenderingIntent.saturation 

Case# CGColorRenderingIntent.saturation

iOSiPadOSMac CatalystmacOStvOSvisionOSwatchOS

```
case saturation
```

## [Discussion](/documentation/coregraphics/cgcolorrenderingintent/saturation#Discussion)

Preserve the relative saturation value of the colors when converting into the gamut of the output device. The result is an image with bright, saturated colors. Saturation intent is good for reproducing images with low detail, such as presentation charts and graphs.

## [See Also](/documentation/coregraphics/cgcolorrenderingintent/saturation#see-also)

### [Constants](/documentation/coregraphics/cgcolorrenderingintent/saturation#Constants)

[`case defaultIntent`](/documentation/coregraphics/cgcolorrenderingintent/defaultintent)The default rendering intent for the graphics context.[`case absoluteColorimetric`](/documentation/coregraphics/cgcolorrenderingintent/absolutecolorimetric)[`case relativeColorimetric`](/documentation/coregraphics/cgcolorrenderingintent/relativecolorimetric)[`case perceptual`](/documentation/coregraphics/cgcolorrenderingintent/perceptual)Preserve the visual relationship between colors by compressing the gamut of the graphics context to fit inside the gamut of the output device. Perceptual intent is good for photographs and other complex, detailed images.
